text_block_list.text:
My mind dwells only on Manhattan Cafe's immense ability. I decide to
make my nightly sojourn to the track once more, when...
Bzzt.
The lights in my office go out.

It saves me the trouble of having to hit all the switches myself at
least. I leave the pitch-black room behind.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: Pant, pant... Once more, and this time...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: This time... I'll catch up to you...

It appears that she is running after that "friend" of hers this evening as
well.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: Tch... So far...

Unlike before, though, her steps are erratic. Maybe she's too fixated
on catching up to her "friend" to maintain a stable pace...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: Pant, pant, pant... I'm still... not there...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: Oh.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: ...Good evening.
choice_data_list:
I know this is sudden, but if it's not too much
trouble...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: Huh?
choice_data_list: Can you tell me about how your friend runs?

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: But... why? ...You can't even...

I can't see her. But it's for that very reason that I want to know more.
What style is she using? When does she make for the finish line?

If I knew what kind of technique her rival was employing, then I'd be
able to offer her some advice. She's trying so hard. I really want to do
what I can for her...
choice_data_list:
It may be none of my business, but I'd like to
help.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: My friend is... very fast...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: ...Yes.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: ...Yes.
Something tells me she's not too keen on opening up about this.

For the next few evenings, I make it a habit to visit the training
course.

Something strange happens every time. Books falling off shelves, or
the TV seemingly turning itself on... I even encounter a jammed door
once more...
choice_data_list: I've gotten used to it, though!

text_block_list.text: Luckily, Manhattan Cafe seems to be getting used to my visits as well.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: Um... She's a bit wild today... Like she's really motivated.

Though she is still a bit apprehensive, she shares a little more about
her friend with me on each passing night.

text_block_list.name: New Trainer A
text_block_list.text:
I'm really not having much luck with this whole scouting thing. How
about you?

My evenings spent with Manhattan Cafe have all served to make me
want to be her trainer.

The only catch is—I can't see her friend, and I don't know if that'll be
a deal-breaker.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: I got some good advice yesterday...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
That trainer... probably thinks I'm strange. And the advice might just
be an excuse to watch...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: ...But... if I'm being honest...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: I feel like... even by a little bit... just a little bit...

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: I've... been able to close the distance between us.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
...
Maybe... I can count on a visit... tonight as well.

I fully intend to watch her train this evening, but I can't help but
wonder if I'm starting to become a nuisance.
Clack.
The door opens without issue...
But I feel as if I'm dragged through it by some immense force.
I quickly brush it off and make for the track.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: Good evening. You're here... a bit earlier than usual...
I can sense a hint of relief in her voice.

The next day, I go out in search of Manhattan Cafe at the courses
once again. I'm greeted by a different face instead.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Oho, searching for Cafe, are we? You're not like most of the other
trainers, then.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Hehe. Why don't you come with me instead? Your mannerisms
fascinate me.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: Isn't that...

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Welcome to my laboratory! Impressive, is it not? Oh, and the other
half is Cafe's space.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
I would urge you not to touch anything over there. I rummaged
through her articles once, and the amount of research material that
spontaneously combusted in the aftermath was enough to bring me to
tears.

What a strange space. Though I can observe it with my own eyes, it
feels as if it lies within a separate dimension entirely.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Perhaps if I were a psychologist, I would be more keen on unraveling
its mysteries.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Tell me, though. What do you think of this "friend" that Cafe is always
speaking of? Do you believe she exists?
choice_data_list: Yes, because Cafe says she does.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
I see. "Existence" itself begs definition, but setting that aside—you
trust in that which neither you nor I, nor anyone else can physically
observe.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Not very scientific of you, I must say. What gives you such
confidence?

Merely shifting your position can change your field of view. Surely it
follows that things can exist outside our perception, visible only to
another.

Thinking this argument to be ironclad, I express it to Agnes
Tachyon...

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Ahahahaha! Wonderful! Even I cannot deny that there is a plethora of
possibilities yet unknown!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: Hehehe. I trust now that you won't be a waste of my time.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
I'm feeling quite invigorated indeed. Come, let me prepare you some
tea. What? Don't fuss over the incandescent glow!

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: So even without seeing my friend, that trainer...
